author: Digitally
text:
Derive .balls runtime paths from one canonical table

bl tracked its internal .balls/ runtime paths in two independent hand-maintained lists: review_safety::RUNTIME_PATHS (the bl review squash backstop) and store_init::ensure_main_gitignore's wanted list (what bl init writes to .gitignore). They overlapped but were not equal, kept aligned only by a // Kept in sync comment — so every new sidecar had to be remembered in both places, and .balls/code-refs shipped half-wired and needed retrofitting.

Introduce src/runtime_paths.rs: one RuntimePath table, each row carrying in_stealth (does the path exist in stealth mode) and backstop (does the squash backstop cover it). Both consumers now derive their sets from it — backstop_paths() for review_safety, gitignore_paths(is_stealth) for store_init. Adding a sidecar is one row; the sync comment is deleted because the invariant is structural now.

Pure refactor: the derived sets are byte-identical to the prior lists (runtime_paths_tests.rs pins them). .balls-worktrees is folded in as a table row flagged backstop:false — it is the parent of the work worktrees, never a subpath of one, so a work/<id> squash cannot reach it; gitignoring it is the whole defense. .balls/state-repo (landed concurrently by bl-c439) is carried as a backstop:true row.
